22.reason: [The variable 'ch' is declared as char, but fgetc() returns an int. When EOF (typically -1) is returned, storing it in a char may cause it to become positive (due to sign extension or unsigned char), leading to an infinite loop. 'ch' should be declared as int.]

When EOF (typically -1) is returned, storing it in a char may cause it to become positive (due to sign extension or unsigned char), leading to an infinite loop. 'ch' should be declared as int.]
